title: 37. Sudoku Solver
question:
Write a program to solve a Sudoku puzzle by filling the empty cells.

A sudoku solution must satisfy all of the following rules:

  1. Each of the digits 1-9 must occur exactly once in each row.
  2. Each of the digits 1-9 must occur exactly once in each column.
  3. Each of the the digits 1-9 must occur exactly once in each of the 9 3x3 sub-boxes of the grid.

Empty cells are indicated by the character '.'.


A sudoku puzzle...


...and its solution numbers marked in red.

Note:

  • The given board contain only digits 1-9 and the character '.'.
  • You may assume that the given Sudoku puzzle will have a single unique solution.
  • The given board size is always 9x9.
code: class Solution(object):\u000A    def solveSudoku(self, board):\u000A        get_hash \u003D lambda x, y, num: {(1, x, int(num)), (2, y, int(num)), (3, x/3 * 3 + y/3, int(num))}\u000A        self.visited \u003D set(loc for x in range(9)\u000A                               for y, num in enumerate(board[x])\u000A                               if num !\u003D \u0027.\u0027\u000A                               for loc in get_hash(x, y, num))\u000A        self.done \u003D False\u000A        def solve(i):\u000A            if not self.done:\u000A                if i \u003E 80: self.done \u003D True\u000A                else:\u000A                    x, y \u003D divmod(i, 9)\u000A                    num \u003D board[x][y]\u000A                    if num \u003D\u003D \u0027.\u0027:\u000A                        for cand in range(1, 10):\u000A                            if not (self.visited \u0026 get_hash(x, y, cand)):\u000A                                self.visited |\u003D get_hash(x, y, cand)\u000A                                board[x][y] \u003D str(cand)\u000A                                solve(i + 1)\u000A                                if not self.done:\u000A                                    board[x][y] \u003D \u0027.\u0027\u000A                                    self.visited \u002D\u003D get_hash(x, y, cand)\u000A                    else:\u000A                        solve(i + 1)\u000A        solve(0)\u000A        return board\u000A                        \u000A                                      \u000A
